foundation.title: CLASS
foundation.subtitle: Blueprints
foundation.content: A Java Class is a blueprint. In Java, objects are created on the 'Heap' using the 'new' keyword.
foundation.scenario: A Blueprints for a Car. It defines 'Color' and 'Speed'. You can't drive the blueprint; you build a physical car (Object) from it.

examples.title: The Template
examples.explanation: Creating a new class structure using the 'class' keyword.
examples.code: class Car { int hp; void drive(); }

examples.title: Encapsulate
examples.explanation: Hiding certain data variables using private so they stay safe from outside code.

examples.title: The Instance
examples.explanation: Building an actual object 'in memory' by calling its constructor.

foundation.title: METHODS
foundation.subtitle: Class Skills
foundation.content: Methods define what an object can 'DO'. They must specify exactly what they return (like int) or 'void'.
foundation.scenario: A Robot. The 'Walk' method is a skill the robot has (Action). You call the robot to use its skill (Call).

examples.title: The Public
examples.explanation: A skill that any part of the program can ask this object to use.

examples.title: The Private
examples.explanation: A internal skill (like 'Heartbeat') that the object keeps to itself.

examples.title: The Param
examples.explanation: Passing information into a skill (like 'Walk 10 Steps').

foundation.title: INHERIT
foundation.subtitle: Polymorphism
foundation.content: Inheritance (extends) allows a 'Child' class to learn all the skills of its 'Parent' and add its own amazing powers.
foundation.scenario: A General Phone vs a Smart Phone. Every smart phone is a phone (extends), but it adds apps and internet on top.

examples.title: The Extends
examples.explanation: Creating a new subclass that takes everything from its base parent class.

examples.title: Override
examples.explanation: Changing how an inherited method behaves in the child version.

examples.title: The Poly
examples.explanation: Using a parent's name to hold any of its children objects flexibly.

foundation.keyPoint: One class extends exactly ONE other class in Java.
